summary: Neurobotics (European AI-powered logistics robotics company based in Germany) is a private investment opportunity — quiet, well-funded, $100M revenue, operating outside the high-visibility Silicon Valley ecosystem.
summary_extended: Friedberg highlights Neurobotics, a German AI-powered logistics robotics company, as a compelling private investment opportunity flying under the radar relative to high-profile US-centric AI robotics plays. He cites the company's $100M in revenue, strong execution, and backing from large institutional investors as evidence of its credibility and maturity. The contrarian appeal lies in the fact that it operates outside the Silicon Valley ecosystem, meaning it has not attracted the same valuation premiums or media attention as comparable US peers. Because Neurobotics is a private company with no US-listed equity, there is no directly actionable public market trade implied by this thesis — it is relevant only to investors with access to private markets.
detail: Friedberg names Neurobotics as a private company pick. It is described as an AI-powered logistics robotics company based in Germany, with large investors, $100M revenue, and strong execution. The appeal is that it is under the radar relative to US-centric AI robotics plays.
confidence_evidence: Named explicitly as a specific pick by Friedberg with revenue and investor detail, but no confirmation of ownership or deep diligence shared.
quote_fragment: Neurobotics in Europe AI powered logistics robotics
